commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e38b0e7b052a7c353d6fb6a01ca1a082264550b0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves Codex runtime automatic compaction ownership to native Codex by skipping OpenClaw preflight/CLI/context-engine compaction paths for Codex, while forwarding explicit compaction to native `thread/compact/start` and updating focused tests plus changelog.

PR surface: Source -312, Tests -597, Docs 0. Total -909 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is present. Source inspection and the PR's focused tests show the current-main fallback and preflight paths being changed, but there is no real Codex app-server session proof.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled PR needs contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of compatibility/availability risk; automation cannot supply the missing real-session evidence.

target.title: Fail Codex compaction at the Codex boundary
target.body:
## Summary

Codex runtime compaction was still being treated like an OpenClaw-owned PI safeguard in a few places. OpenClaw could preflight-compact Codex reply turns, post-turn compact Codex CLI transcripts, let a context-engine plugin replace Codex app-server compaction, and force plugin compaction before or after Codex `turn/start` overflow handling.

That is the wrong boundary. Codex already owns automatic compaction inside the Codex runtime. OpenClaw should not predict when a Codex thread needs compaction and then rewrite the transcript around it. At best that duplicates the native harness. At worst it crosses runtime/auth boundaries, restarts the shared app-server, or drifts a Codex-backed OpenAI session into OpenClaw/public Responses summarization.

This tightens the contract. Automatic compaction for Codex runtime sessions is native Codex-owned. OpenClaw now skips its preflight and CLI automatic compaction paths for Codex, removes the Codex app-server context-engine forced-compaction paths, and no longer lets an `ownsCompaction` context engine take over Codex app-server compaction. Explicit compaction is still supported: `/compact` and plugin-requested/manual compaction requests are forwarded to Codex as native `thread/compact/start`, and context-engine maintenance can run afterward when native compaction succeeds.

The fail-closed behavior from the earlier patch stays: if native Codex compaction cannot run or cannot complete, OpenClaw reports that failure honestly. It does not restart the shared Codex app-server as a retry strategy, and it does not fall back to OpenClaw/public OpenAI compaction for Codex runtime sessions. Non-Codex pinned harnesses still keep their previous context-engine fallback path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex-runtime sessions should not be automatically compacted by OpenClaw preflight, CLI, context-engine, or public OpenAI summarization paths. Explicit/manual/plugin compaction should still forward to native Codex, and native Codex compaction failures should fail closed.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout using the Codex app-server compaction seam, Codex app-server turn-start lifecycle, reply preflight compaction seam, CLI compaction lifecycle, embedded runner compaction seam, and docs/changelog checks.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `pnpm check:changed`; `pnpm build`; `pnpm lint:docs`.

Evidence after fix: App-server tests now prove manual/plugin compaction forwards to native `thread/compact/start` even when a context engine advertises `ownsCompaction`, and missing native bindings fail instead of delegating to that engine. Reply preflight tests now prove Codex runtime sessions return unchanged even with over-threshold token snapshots. CLI tests now prove Codex runtime sessions skip OpenClaw automatic CLI compaction entirely, while non-Codex pinned harness fallback still works. Run-attempt lifecycle tests now prove Codex turn-start overflow recovery does not call plugin compaction or wait on a hung context-engine compact call.

Observed result after fix: Targeted tests, changed checks, formatting, docs lint, diff whitespace checks, and build all passed locally.

What was not tested: I did not force a destructive live compaction timeout on Pash's running dev agent session.
target.author: pashpashpash

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
